commands.name: /chat
commands.description: Load a previous session or start a new one

commands.name: /context
commands.description: Manage context files or show token usage

commands.name: /effort
commands.description: Set thinking effort for this session

commands.name: /feedback
commands.description: Submit feedback, request features, or report issues

commands.name: /goal
commands.description: Set a goal with validation criteria for iterative completion

commands.name: /guide
commands.description: Get help with Kiro CLI features from the guide agent

commands.name: /plan
commands.description: Switch to Plan agent for breaking down ideas into implementation plans

commands.name: /reply
commands.description: Open editor pre-filled with the last assistant message to compose a reply

commands.name: /rewind
commands.description: Rewind conversation to a previous turn (forks into a new session)

commands.name: /stats
commands.description: Show request IDs and timings for debugging slow turns

prompts.name: claude-mem-curate
prompts.description: 把 claude-mem 抽出的 shortlist 精選成少量繁體中文、去重後寫進 AIMemory。當使用者說「精選/整理 claude-mem」「把最近重點存記憶」,或每日自動 ingestion 時使用。

prompts.name: dual-skill-review-loop
prompts.description: Use when producing any deliverable that needs quality assurance — writing, reports, proposals, code designs — and you want the output to reach ≥95 score through structured self-review iterations

prompts.name: excel-to-ai-doc
prompts.description: Convert image-heavy Excel game spec spreadsheets (.xlsx) into an AI-readable knowledge structure — Markdown text + extracted original images (anchored to their source cells) + metadata.json. Use when the user wants to turn a 規格書/spec/design xlsx into something an AI or agent can read, asks to "convert this spec to markdown", "make this xlsx AI-readable", "extract images and tables from this spreadsheet for analysis", or needs to feed a slot/game spec workbook to a vision model. Triggers on game design specs, paytables, UI/flow screenshots embedded in .xlsx. NOT for clean tabular data exports (use a plain csv/markdown converter) or non-xlsx files.

prompts.name: huashu-slides
prompts.description: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。

prompts.name: knowhow-accumulation
prompts.description: Use when capturing lessons from AI failures into structured ❌→✅ records, when reviewing past interactions to extract reusable patterns, or when building a self-improving knowledge base from repeated mistakes

prompts.name: memory-to-skill
prompts.description: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-scheduled-prompts
prompts.description: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-text-token-signaling
prompts.description: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用

prompts.name: ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail
prompts.description: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用

prompts.name: ms-portable-skill-authoring
prompts.description: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用

prompts.name: ms-streaming-token-pipeline
prompts.description: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol
prompts.description: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival
prompts.description: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用

prompts.name: ms-wiki-knowledge-base
prompts.description: 當 agent bridge 要維運雙層記憶（facts + wiki 知識庫）時使用：執行 wikisync（topic shard 蒸餾成 wiki 頁）、factlint（facts 去重/去瑣碎/衰減/升格偵測）、wikilint（wiki 過時/孤兒/斷連健康檢查），或要為新 bridge 設計同款 wiki 維運流程時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用

prompts.name: non-engineer-agent-design
prompts.description: Use when designing Custom GPT / Claude Project / Gemini Gem instructions for non-engineers, or when helping someone structure their AI workflow using the 5-piece framework (Agent, Skill, Steering, Hook, KnowHow)

prompts.name: self-eval-prompt-pattern
prompts.description: Use when designing prompts or instructions that require AI to self-assess quality before delivering output, when setting up automated quality gates in Custom GPT / Project / Gem instructions, or when AI outputs need measurable pass/fail criteria

prompts.name: skill-creator
prompts.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

prompts.name: sync-fork-from-upstream
prompts.description: 當使用者要把自己的 fork / 下游倉庫同步到原專案（upstream）的最新更新時使用。觸發語包含「同步原專案」「拿上游更新」「sync upstream」「更新原專案」「原本的倉庫有更新怎麼拉」。涵蓋一次性 upstream remote 設定、半自動同步流程（fetch → 拋棄式分支試合併 → 型別檢查 → 停在衝突/失敗、絕不自動 push）、以及 fork 與 upstream 無共同歷史時的「重新接血脈」做法。

prompts.name: uk-slot-codegen
prompts.description: UK slot codegen 統一入口。使用者提供規格路徑+目標專案路徑，自動串接 adapter→codegen→validation 全流程。支援新專案/增量更新/僅驗證三種模式，每步 checkpoint 可中斷恢復。

prompts.name: uk-slot-extrabet
prompts.description: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。

prompts.name: uk-slot-fake-reel-manager
prompts.description: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充

prompts.name: uk-slot-spec-to-impl
prompts.description: 當拿到一份 UK 老虎機遊戲規格書（xlsx）要開發新遊戲、使用者說「從規格書實作」「開新的 slot 專案」「規格書轉開發規格」、或要為新遊戲起專案/拆任務時使用。涵蓋 xlsx 轉換、差異開發規格、milestone 任務拆解三步驟。

prompts.name: uk-slot-state-machine
prompts.description: UK 老虎機狀態機設定，新增、移除或修改 SetStateMachine() 遊戲狀態時觸發。

prompts.name: vc-kiro-delegate
prompts.description: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。

prompts.name: writing-skills
prompts.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

tools.name: google_docs_append
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Append plain text to the end of a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_docs_create
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Create a Google Doc, optionally with initial text.

tools.name: google_docs_get
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Read a Google Doc's title and plain text.

tools.name: google_docs_replace_text
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Replace all matching plain text in a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_drive_search_docs
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Drive for Google Docs by title.

tools.name: google_maps_geocode
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Convert an address or place description to coordinates.

tools.name: google_maps_route
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Compute a route between two addresses or named places.

tools.name: google_maps_search_places
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Places by natural-language query.

prompts.description: 當 agent bridge 要維運雙層記憶（facts + wiki 知識庫）時使用：執行 wikisync（topic shard 蒸餾成 wiki 頁）、factlint（facts 去重/去瑣碎/衰減/升格偵測）、wikilint（wiki 過時/孤兒/斷連健康檢查），或要為新 bridge 設計同款 wiki 維運流程時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-exit-code-false-positive
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中跑 git clone、pip install、npm install、winget 等命令時，明明工作完成卻收到 exit code 1、stderr 有進度條或 notice、shell tool 回報失敗時使用

prompts.name: ms-windows-shell-interactive-prompt
prompts.description: 當在 Windows PowerShell 或 cmd.exe 中執行讀檔、列目錄、搜尋指令（Get-Content、type、cat、more）導致 tool call 卡住不回、或在 Kiro agent 與 ACP bridge 裡看到 PowerShell 的互動式參數詢問（例如 Path 參數提示）時使用

prompts.name: non-engineer-agent-design
prompts.description: Use when designing Custom GPT / Claude Project / Gemini Gem instructions for non-engineers, or when helping someone structure their AI workflow using the 5-piece framework (Agent, Skill, Steering, Hook, KnowHow)

prompts.name: self-eval-prompt-pattern
prompts.description: Use when designing prompts or instructions that require AI to self-assess quality before delivering output, when setting up automated quality gates in Custom GPT / Project / Gem instructions, or when AI outputs need measurable pass/fail criteria

prompts.name: skill-creator
prompts.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

prompts.name: sync-fork-from-upstream
prompts.description: 當使用者要把自己的 fork / 下游倉庫同步到原專案（upstream）的最新更新時使用。觸發語包含「同步原專案」「拿上游更新」「sync upstream」「更新原專案」「原本的倉庫有更新怎麼拉」。涵蓋一次性 upstream remote 設定、半自動同步流程（fetch → 拋棄式分支試合併 → 型別檢查 → 停在衝突/失敗、絕不自動 push）、以及 fork 與 upstream 無共同歷史時的「重新接血脈」做法。

prompts.name: uk-slot-codegen
prompts.description: UK slot codegen 統一入口。使用者提供規格路徑+目標專案路徑，自動串接 adapter→codegen→validation 全流程。支援新專案/增量更新/僅驗證三種模式，每步 checkpoint 可中斷恢復。

prompts.name: uk-slot-extrabet
prompts.description: UK 老虎機 ExtraBet（額外押注/加倍投注）子系統指南。建立、修改、除錯 ExtraBet 功能，或處理 unshow/replay 還原、單倍率單步驟 / 多倍率兩步驟跳窗變體、多倍率 type 時觸發。涵蓋 framework ExtraBetComponent / extraBetController、專案 ExtraBetWindow 跳窗、ExtraBetEffectManager 動畫、SlotReels 假轉輪整合、GameView 整合多方關係。

prompts.name: uk-slot-fake-reel-manager
prompts.description: FakeReelManager 假轉輪帶建立、修改、檢查或擴充

prompts.name: uk-slot-spec-to-impl
prompts.description: 當拿到一份 UK 老虎機遊戲規格書（xlsx）要開發新遊戲、使用者說「從規格書實作」「開新的 slot 專案」「規格書轉開發規格」、或要為新遊戲起專案/拆任務時使用。涵蓋 xlsx 轉換、差異開發規格、milestone 任務拆解三步驟。

prompts.name: uk-slot-state-machine
prompts.description: UK 老虎機狀態機設定，新增、移除或修改 SetStateMachine() 遊戲狀態時觸發。

prompts.name: vc-kiro-delegate
prompts.description: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。

prompts.name: writing-skills
prompts.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

tools.name: google_docs_append
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Append plain text to the end of a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_docs_create
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Create a Google Doc, optionally with initial text.

tools.name: google_docs_get
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Read a Google Doc's title and plain text.

tools.name: google_docs_replace_text
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Replace all matching plain text in a Google Doc.

tools.name: google_drive_search_docs
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Drive for Google Docs by title.

tools.name: google_maps_geocode
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Convert an address or place description to coordinates.

tools.name: google_maps_route
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Compute a route between two addresses or named places.

tools.name: google_maps_search_places
tools.description: (MCP tool from google) Search Google Places by natural-language query.

commands.name: /chat
commands.description: Load a previous session or start a new one

commands.name: /context
commands.description: Manage context files or show token usage

commands.name: /effort
commands.description: Set thinking effort for this session

commands.name: /feedback
commands.description: Submit feedback, request features, or report issues

commands.name: /goal
commands.description: Set a goal with validation criteria for iterative completion

commands.name: /guide
commands.description: Get help with Kiro CLI features from the guide agent

commands.name: /plan
commands.description: Switch to Plan agent for breaking down ideas into implementation plans

commands.name: /reply
commands.description: Open editor pre-filled with the last assistant message to compose a reply

commands.name: /rewind
commands.description: Rewind conversation to a previous turn (forks into a new session)

commands.name: /stats
commands.description: Show request IDs and timings for debugging slow turns

prompts.name: claude-mem-curate
prompts.description: 把 claude-mem 抽出的 shortlist 精選成少量繁體中文、去重後寫進 AIMemory。當使用者說「精選/整理 claude-mem」「把最近重點存記憶」,或每日自動 ingestion 時使用。

prompts.name: dual-skill-review-loop
prompts.description: Use when producing any deliverable that needs quality assurance — writing, reports, proposals, code designs — and you want the output to reach ≥95 score through structured self-review iterations

prompts.name: excel-to-ai-doc
prompts.description: Convert image-heavy Excel game spec spreadsheets (.xlsx) into an AI-readable knowledge structure — Markdown text + extracted original images (anchored to their source cells) + metadata.json. Use when the user wants to turn a 規格書/spec/design xlsx into something an AI or agent can read, asks to "convert this spec to markdown", "make this xlsx AI-readable", "extract images and tables from this spreadsheet for analysis", or needs to feed a slot/game spec workbook to a vision model. Triggers on game design specs, paytables, UI/flow screenshots embedded in .xlsx. NOT for clean tabular data exports (use a plain csv/markdown converter) or non-xlsx files.

prompts.name: huashu-slides
prompts.description: AI演示文稿全流程制作：内容结构化→设计选型→AI插画/HTML构建→PPTX导出。17种实战验证的视觉风格（漫画/极简/数据叙事/国风），可编辑HTML与全AI视觉两条路径自由选择。当用户提到"做PPT"、"制作幻灯片"、"演示文稿"、"Keynote"、"slides"、"课件"、"汇报材料"、"路演"时使用。

prompts.name: knowhow-accumulation
prompts.description: Use when capturing lessons from AI failures into structured ❌→✅ records, when reviewing past interactions to extract reusable patterns, or when building a self-improving knowledge base from repeated mistakes

prompts.name: memory-to-skill
prompts.description: 當使用者要求「檢視 session 資料夾並把內容整理成 skill」、需要從過去對話中抽取可重用模式、或要把剛剛完成的工作經驗固化成 skill 時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-scheduled-prompts
prompts.description: 當需要為 LLM agent bridge（Telegram bot、Slack bot、CLI UI）做延遲 / 常態性 prompt 觸發、排程 bridge 指令（如每天凌晨 /restart）、或要在 agent 回覆裡嵌 token 讓使用者自助建排程時使用

prompts.name: ms-agent-text-token-signaling
prompts.description: 當 LLM agent 需要觸發 host 端動作（傳檔、開網址、執行指令）但協定不允許 agent 端發起自訂 RPC、或想在 streaming 文字回覆中加上 side effect 又不破壞聊天 UX 時使用

prompts.name: ms-kiro-strreplace-silent-fail
prompts.description: 當用 Kiro 的 strReplace 或 fs-edit 類工具改檔、tool 回報 Successfully replaced N occurrences 但事後 grep / read 發現磁碟內容沒變、或同一個檔案連續發幾個 strReplace 只有部分生效時使用

prompts.name: ms-portable-skill-authoring
prompts.description: 當寫 skill 會被多個 agent CLI（Kiro / Codex / Claude）或多台機器共用、要避免把 F:\AI\AIMemory 或 ~/.kiro 這類絕對路徑寫死在 SKILL.md、或要讓 bridge 把環境差異注入成變數讓同一份 SKILL.md 跨機器重用時使用

prompts.name: ms-streaming-token-pipeline
prompts.description: Use when a Telegram/Slack bot streams text via mid-turn edits plus a final render. Both paths must share one transform, or ASK buttons drop, half-tokens leak, and rate-limit recovery overwrites it.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-ask-button-protocol
prompts.description: Use when emitting <<ASK:...>> for Telegram bridge buttons, or when an ASK token renders verbatim. Label soft cap 20 chars; >20 SHALL use numbered body list with short labels. See SKILL.md for limits.

prompts.name: ms-telegram-bot-rate-limit-survival
prompts.description: 當 Telegram bot 對使用者 chat 串 streaming edit 或連發多則訊息、遇到 429 Too Many Requests / retry_after 愈累積愈長（60s→183s）、grammY autoRetry 超過 maxDelaySeconds 拋錯、或需要設計「限流期間凍結 UI + 限流結束後自動補發」時使用

prompts.description: 將實作任務委派給 Kiro CLI（headless mode + claude-opus-4.6）執行，把主 agent 的 Claude Max 5x 配額（5 小時 message cap）保留給協調與決策，把實作丟給 Kiro 的不限量訂閱算力。Claude Code 負責預估、規劃、light review；Kiro 負責具體實作與第一輪 self-review。當用戶說「用 kiro 做」「交給 kiro」「省 token」「叫 kiro 寫」時務必使用此 skill；當主 agent 自己評估某個任務「預估會花 2k+ tokens」時也要主動委派 — 例如新增函式、寫測試、重構函式、實作模組。即使用戶沒明說「kiro」，只要預估產出規模 ≥2k tokens，就主動委派並在訊息中告知預估值。

prompts.name: writing-skills
prompts.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment

tools.name: code
tools.description:
Code intelligence with AST parsing and fuzzy search. Language auto-detected from file extension.

CORE FEATURES:
• Fuzzy search for symbols (classes, functions, methods)
• Extracts function/class signatures via AST
• Structural AST search and rewrite (ast-grep)
• Codebase overview and directory exploration

NOTE: LSP operations (find_references, goto_definition, get_hover, get_completions, get_diagnostics, rename_symbol) require LSP initialization.

## Available Operations
- search_symbols: Find symbol definitions by name
- lookup_symbols: Batch lookup specific symbols
- get_document_symbols: List all symbols in a file
- pattern_search: AST-based structural search
- pattern_rewrite: AST-based code transformation
- generate_codebase_overview: High-level codebase structure
- search_codebase_map: Focused directory exploration

tools.name: glob
tools.description:
Find files and directories whose paths match a glob pattern. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Finding files by name pattern (e.g., "*.rs", "**/*.tsx")
- Discovering project structure
- Listing files in specific directories

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a glob pattern to match files
- Optionally specify a root directory to search from
- Optionally specify a limit on results and max depth

PATTERNS:
- "*.rs" - All .rs files in current directory
- "**/*.rs" - All .rs files recursively
- "src/**/*.{ts,tsx}" - All TypeScript files under src/

tools.name: goal
tools.description:
Signal goal completion. A goal is a binding completion contract — the system will continue re-prompting you until the contract is satisfied.

Commands:
- complete: Certify that ALL success criteria defined in the goal have been met, verified by concrete evidence you produced during this session (test output, file contents, command results). Your summary must cite the specific evidence that satisfies each criterion. A completion without cited verification will not satisfy the contract.

COMPLETION STANDARD (treat as binding):
1. Each criterion in the goal MUST be individually verified by tool output you can cite.
2. Belief, assumption, or narrative confidence is NOT evidence. Only tool results count.
3. If ANY criterion lacks verifiable evidence, you MUST NOT call complete. Continue working.
4. If a verification path is blocked, try alternative approaches before giving up. Only after exhausting alternatives should you end your turn without calling complete — the system will re-prompt you on the next iteration with a chance to try differently.
5. If across multiple iterations you identify a genuinely impossible impediment (same hard blocker confirmed repeatedly despite different approaches), you MAY call complete with a summary that documents: what was accomplished, what is blocked, the evidence of the blocker, and what would unblock it. This is the ONLY path to early completion without full success — and requires cited evidence of the blocker itself.

STRATEGY:
- Divide complex goals into independent sub-tasks. Use sub-agents to parallelize work when the goal has separable components (e.g., implement + test, frontend + backend).
- When available, delegate verification to a sub-agent acting as an independent auditor. An external reviewer that inspects the work product without sharing your context produces stronger evidence of completion than self-assessment.
- Prefer divide-and-conquer: decompose, delegate, verify independently, then synthesize.

tools.name: grep
tools.description:
Fast text pattern search in files using regex. Respects .gitignore.

WHEN TO USE:
- Searching for literal text patterns, error messages, TODOs, config values
- Finding files containing specific text

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- For semantic code understanding, use the code tool instead
- For finding symbol definitions or usages, use the code tool

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a regex pattern to search for
- Optionally specify a path to search from (defaults to current directory)
- Optionally specify a file filter glob (e.g., "*.rs", "*.{ts,tsx}")

OUTPUT MODES:
- content: Show matching lines with file path and line number (default)
- files_with_matches: Only show file paths that contain matches
- count: Show count of matches per file

tools.name: introspect
tools.description:
Look up documentation about this chat application's own features, slash commands, settings, or capabilities.

WHEN TO USE:
- User asks about this assistant's features, commands, or settings
- User wants to know what slash commands are available
- User asks how to use a specific feature of this chat application

WHEN NOT TO USE:
- General coding questions, AWS help, or tasks the user wants you to perform
- Questions unrelated to this chat application itself

HOW TO USE:
- Provide a query to search the documentation
- Or provide a doc_path to retrieve a specific document
- When mentioning commands in your response, always prefix them with '/' (e.g., '/chat save', '/chat load', '/context')
- CRITICAL: Only provide information explicitly documented. If details are not documented, clearly state the information is not available rather than generating assumptions.

tools.name: knowledge
tools.description:
A tool for indexing and searching content across chat sessions using semantic search.

## Overview
This tool enables persistent storage and retrieval of information using semantic search (MiniLLM) or keyword search (BM25). Content remains available across sessions for later use.

## When to use
- When users ask to query your knowledge bases or kbs
- When you need to search previously indexed content
- When users request to index new content (code, markdown, CSV, PDF, and other text file formats)
- When exploring unfamiliar content to find relevant information
- When users ask about topics that might be in indexed knowledge bases

## When not to use
- When content has not been indexed yet and user hasn't requested indexing
- When you need real-time or external information not in the knowledge base

## Notes
- Use 'show' command to list available knowledge bases before searching
- Search can target specific knowledge bases (context_id) or all knowledge bases
- Use default limit values unless specifically needed; fewer results for focused search
- Pagination available via offset parameter for large result sets
- 'add' command indexes new content; 'update' command refreshes existing knowledge bases
- Unless there is a clear reason to modify the search query, use the user's original wording for better semantic matching

tools.name: read
tools.description:
Tool for reading files, directories and images. Always provide an 'operations' array.

For single operation: provide array with one element.
For batch operations: provide array with multiple elements.

Available modes:
- Line: Read lines from a file
- Directory: List directory contents
- Image: Read and process images

Examples:
1. Single: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file.txt"}]}
2. Batch: {"operations": [{"mode": "Line", "path": "/file1.txt"}, {"mode": "Directory", "path": "/src"}]}
3. Image: {"operations": [{"mode": "Image", "image_paths": ["/path/to/image.png"]}]}

tools.name: shell
tools.description:
A tool for executing shell commands.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use only as a last-resort when no other available tool can accomplish the task

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the command to execute

FEATURES:

LIMITATIONS:
- Executes commands using the detected shell on Windows (PowerShell or cmd.exe)

TIPS:
- Use the fileRead and fileWrite tools for reading and modifying files

tools.name: subagent
tools.description:
Spawn and coordinate multiple AI agents in a pipeline (DAG). Each stage runs as a
persistent session. Stages with no depends_on start immediately in parallel.

MODES:
- background (not yet implemented): Fire-and-forget. Returns immediately, results arrive in inbox.
- blocking (default): Waits for all stages to complete, returns consolidated results.

USE THIS when you need multi-step work with specialized agents:
- Research → Implement → Review pipelines
- Parallel research tracks that feed into a single implementer
- Any workflow where stages have dependencies

LOOPS:
- Add loop_to on a stage to create iterative cycles (e.g., reviewer loops back to implementer)
- trigger: text in the stage's output that triggers the loop (e.g., "NEEDS_CHANGES")
- max_iterations: safety cap to prevent infinite loops
- The target stage re-runs with the triggering stage's feedback as context

Each stage becomes a session you can monitor via ctrl+g in the TUI.

tools.name: todo_list
tools.description: A tool for creating a task list and keeping track of tasks. This tool should be requested EVERY time the user gives you a task that will take multiple steps. A task list should be made BEFORE executing any steps. Steps should be marked off AS YOU COMPLETE THEM. DO NOT display your own tasks or task list AT ANY POINT; this is done for you. Complete the tasks in the same order that you provide them. If the user tells you to skip a step, DO NOT mark it as completed.

tools.name: use_aws
tools.description: Make an AWS CLI api call with the specified service, operation, and parameters. All arguments MUST conform to the AWS CLI specification. Should the output of the invocation indicate a malformed command, invoke help to obtain the the correct command.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch and extract content from a specific URL. Supports three modes: 'selective' (default, extracts relevant sections around search terms), 'truncated' (first 8000 chars), 'full' (complete content).

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: WebSearch looks up information that is outside the model's training data or cannot be reliably inferred from the current codebase/context.

tools.name: write
tools.description:
A tool for creating and editing text files.

WHEN TO USE THIS TOOL:
- Use when you need to create a new file, or modify an existing file
- Perfect for updating text-based file formats

HOW TO USE:
- Provide the path to the file you want to create or modify
- Specify the operation to perform: one of `create`, `strReplace`, or `insert`
- Use `create` to create a new file. Required parameter is `content`. Parent directories will be created if they are missing.
- Use `strReplace` to replace and update the content of an existing file.
- Use `insert` to insert content at a specific line, or append content to the end of a file.

TIPS:
- To append content to the end of a file, use `insert` with no `insert_line`

prompt.text:
[Telegram bridge tool: send file]
`<<SEND_FILE:絕對路徑>>` — 附檔給使用者（圖片≤10MB走photo，餘走document）。路徑須在 TG_SEND_ROOTS 底下。
僅在使用者明確要求傳檔時加；產生/儲存檔案不構成傳檔意圖，回報路徑即可。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: ask with buttons]
`<<ASK:id|key=label|*key=label|...>>`（單行）— 渲染 inline keyboard，點擊回送 `[ASK:id] key`。
限制：id≤32 key≤20 [a-zA-Z0-9_-]；label≤40 不含`|``=`；1-8選項；`*`標推薦（至多一個）。
選項說明長時正文列 1./2./3.，label 只放短標題。違反限制會原封顯示無法渲染。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: schedule]
`<<SCHEDULE:<time>|<prompt>>>`（單行）— 建立延遲/常態性排程。
一次性：`30s`/`15m`/`2h`/`1d`/`HH:MM`/`tomorrow HH:MM`/`MM-DD HH:MM`/`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`。
常態性：`every 30m`/`daily 09:00`/`weekly mon 09:00`/`cron */5 * * * *`。
prompt≤1000字，不可含`>>`。單則至多3個。僅在使用者明確要求排程時使用。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: report skill use]
`<<SKILL_USED:skill-name|reason>>`（單行）— 你參考了某 SKILL.md 時加。
name=資料夾名 kebab-case≤60；reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。一則至多5個，重複去重。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: goal done signal]
`<<GOAL_DONE:reason>>`（單行）— /goal 標的達成時加在回覆最後。
reason≤500 不含`|`或換行。未完成不加。/goal 未啟動時忽略。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: restart]
`<<RESTART:reason>>`（單行）— 重啟 session（context 過大、工具異常、需刷新 preamble）。
重要：restart 前先 remember() 存工作狀態，格式：
  - `[WS] task: <目標>` — 當前高層任務
  - `[WS] completed: <已完成項>` — 本 session 完成的事（可多條）
  - `[WS] blocked: <阻塞項>` — 等待中的事
  - `[WS] key_refs: <重要路徑或值>` — 需帶到下個 session 的參考
  - `[WS] next_action: <下一步>` — 恢復後第一件要做的事
增量存檔：多步驟任務每完成一個里程碑就先 remember() 一條 `[WS] completed:`，
不要等 restart 才存——session 可能非計畫性中斷（crash / idle / force-kill）。
Guardrail：同 /goal 最多5次，間隔≥5分鐘。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: sticker]
`<<STICKER:emotion>>`（單行）— 發送 video sticker。
可用：loading, cry, love, hello, refuse, sad, angry, sleep, happy, shock, agree, clap, ok, sorry, thanks, good。適時使用（打招呼/完成/道歉等），不要每則都加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: wiki query]
`<<WIKI_QUERY:slug|summary>>`（單行）— 標記此回覆值得存為 wiki page。
slug=[a-z0-9_-]≤80；summary≤200字描述主題。Bridge 會自動整理成 wiki/queries/ 頁面。
條件：回覆≥200字 + 跨3+不同主題的綜合性回答。短回覆/操作確認不加。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: propose skill]
`<<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>`（單行）— 提議把本次對話模式寫成 skill。
name=kebab-case≤60（將成為 ms-{name}/ 資料夾名）；reason≤500 說明為何值得。
Bridge 發確認按鈕，使用者同意才萃取。僅在發現可跨專案重用的模式時加，一則至多1個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: continue]
`<<CONTINUE>>`（單行）— 告訴 bridge 你還沒做完，需要再一輪。
Bridge 收到後自動排程下一個 turn，不需使用者介入。
使用時機：單次回覆做不完（tool call 太多、要改多個檔案、多步驟任務）。
限制：/goal 啟動時不需要用（goal 系統自帶 continuation）；一則只認一個。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: specialist proxy]
`<<SPECIALIST_PROXY:name|使用者的原始訊息>>`（單行）— 把使用者的訊息轉交給本機 specialist 即時回答。
進入 proxy 模式後，使用者後續訊息直送 specialist，回覆直送使用者，直到使用者說 /back。
Specialist 的記憶會回流到你的 facts。proxy 結束時你會收到對話摘要。

可用 specialist：
- slot-dev（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/slot-dev）
- researcher（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/researcher）
- general（工作目錄：${BRIDGE_DIR}/specialists/general）

使用時機：使用者的問題明確屬於某 specialist 的領域，且需要即時互動（不是背景任務）。
背景任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；即時對話用 SPECIALIST_PROXY。
[End tool]

[Telegram bridge tool: parallel delegate]
`<<PARALLEL_DELEGATE:task_id|specialist_name|goal prompt>>`（單行）— 並行委派多個任務給 specialist。
同一個 turn 放多個 PARALLEL_DELEGATE，bridge 會同時 spawn 多個獨立 instance 並行執行。
全部完成後結果一次注入你的 session（格式：[PARALLEL_RESULT:task_id]）。

格式規則：
- task_id：唯一識別碼（如 `pt_1`、`pt_2`）
- specialist_name：目標 specialist（同一個 name 可以開多個 instance）
- goal prompt：任務描述（不可含 `>>` 或 `|`），應含五要素：
  1. 目標與動機 2. 已知背景（已掌握的事實/證據） 3. 待決問題 4. 驗收條件 5. 回報格式
- context 模式選擇：
  - 決策型（方案取捨/設計判斷）：自己打包 context brief 進 goal，精簡但完整
  - 調查型（code review/跨檔驗證）：goal 裡給檔案路徑+問題，讓 specialist 自己讀 code 調查
- 每個 task 獨立 instance，互不干擾——但要避免多個 task 改同一個檔案
- ⚠️ 同樣需要至少一個 tool call 防止 CLI 吞 token
- 結果回來後：給 delegate 結論認真權重（獨立視角）；僅在經驗性失敗或一手資料矛盾時才不採納；整合後簡要告知使用者

使用時機：任務可拆成多個獨立子任務且各自耗時 > 1 分鐘（如同時研究多支影片、同時改不同模組）。
單一任務用 RELAY_DELEGATE；可並行的多任務用 PARALLEL_DELEGATE。
[End tool]

[Environment — machine-specific paths the bridge resolved for this session]
Skills and notes that need filesystem paths should reference these variables,
not hard-coded values. This lets the same SKILL.md work on multiple machines.
- MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory
- SKILL_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.kiro\skills
- AGENT_CONFIG_DIR=C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.kiro
- USER_ID=509424983
Conventions: sessions are under ${MEMORY_DIR}/sessions/, archived under
${MEMORY_DIR}/oldSessions/, master fact log at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts-${USER_ID}.md,
topic shards at ${MEMORY_DIR}/facts_Topic/${USER_ID}/<topic>.md.
Agent-wide config files (agent configs) live under
${AGENT_CONFIG_DIR}/.
Specialist 歷史產出存在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/（結構化 JSON），需要時用 read tool 查閱。
[End environment]

[Project context — AI.md layered loading]
工作目錄可能有 `AI.md` 檔案記錄該項目/目錄的上下文要點。
規則：讀到或修改某目錄的檔案時，檢查該目錄及上層（最多 3 層）有無 AI.md。
有就用 read tool 讀取作為工作上下文。同 session 同一個 AI.md 只需讀一次。
多層都有時全部讀（根先子後）。AI.md ≤2000 字，寫架構/慣例/踩坑/測試指引。
主動寫入：在某目錄新增≥3檔案、做架構性改動、或踩坑修復時，主動建立/更新 AI.md。
SPEC：任務≥3工項且跨≥2檔案時先開 SPEC.md；完成工項標 [x]；session 結束前更新進度。
預設行為是寫，不是不寫。詳見 POLICIES/proactive-write-policy.md。
CodeGraph：首次對某專案做修改或追呼叫鏈時，用 codegraph_status(projectPath) 檢查該專案有無索引。
有索引→優先用 codegraph_context/trace/impact 而非手動 grep；改函式簽名前必須先 codegraph_impact。
無索引→回報使用者，建議建立。已知有索引的專案以 codegraph_status 回傳為準。
[End project context]

[User profile — structured identity and preferences]
# User Profile

## 身份
- 遊戲公司（IGS）開發者
- 做 UK 市場老虎機（slot）遊戲
- 技術棧：Cocos Creator 3.6.2 + TypeScript + Astarte Framework
- 個人 AI 專案：telegram-kiro-bridge（Telegram ↔ ACP agent 橋接器）

## 溝通風格
- 繁體中文為主
- 簡潔直接，不需寒暄
- 回覆含選項時用 ASK button，減少手打
- 重要決策先列選項再問，不要代替決定

## 工作偏好
- Git commit message 用中文
- Git commit 前先確認，不逕自 commit
- HTML 報告要有目錄錨點跳轉
- PDF 工作流：HTML+CSS → Playwright headless Chromium
- 進入 UK slot 專案時主動建 AI.md（不限改動檔案數）
- 方法/函式命名用大駝峰（PascalCase）—— 限 UK slot 專案

## Agent 設定偏好
- Steering 雙軌：closed-loop（主 agent 長 session）+ karpathy-guardrails（specialist/短任務）
- Context budget discipline：研究前先估量，偏好精簡策略
- 自我改進優先級：Context Budget > ASK 強制觸發 > 其他
- Skill 正本放 G:\AI\AI-canonical（通用）或 AI-canonical-corp（slot）

## 工作節奏
- 主要工作時間：白天（台灣時區 UTC+8）
- /dream 夜間維運：每日 04:00
- 靜默時段偏好：深夜不打擾
[End user profile]

- [f_bf688a] [2026-07-10T15:54:46.733Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 的 status server 已安全加固（commit c9174e3，需重啟 bridge 生效）：handler 加 async 錯誤邊界+safeDecode 防畸形 URL crash、預設綁 127.0.0.1（新增 STATUS_BIND_HOST env var 可改）、移除 CORS *、detail API 的 env 機密欄位遮罩、preamble 路由改 specialist 白名單擋 path traversal、artifact 檔名改 specialist name 錨定 regex 解析、facts 行解析修正 timestamp 前綴、Live View 取消按鈕依 server 回應顯示
- [f_0e5446] [2026-07-10T15:54:46.744Z] bridge 架構陷阱：index.ts 的全域 unhandledRejection handler 會 process.exit(1)，任何同 process 的 async callback（如 HTTP handler）未捕捉的 throw 都會殺掉整個 bridge——新增 server/handler 必須自帶錯誤邊界
- [f_6fe390] [2026-07-10T15:54:46.749Z] AIMemory artifacts 檔名格式 <date>_<taskId>_<specialist>_<slug>.json 中 taskId 本身可含底線（如 pt_codegen_probe），positional split('_') 解析必錯，須用已知欄位（specialist name）錨定 regex
- [f_76b1f7] [2026-07-10T16:07:35.371Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 的 RELAY_DELEGATE tool note 只在 config.relay 開啟且 relay-peers.json 存在時注入——目前生產 .env relay 欄位空、peers 檔不存在，故 ceiling 護欄與實際 preamble 都不含它；未來啟用 relay（含 peers 清單約 1–1.8k chars）時 fixed core 會逼近 8000 上限，tripwire 觸發屬設計預期，按 check-preamble.mjs 註解調高並在 commit 說明即可
- [f_88d3a1] [2026-07-10T16:07:35.398Z] check-preamble.mjs 量測陷阱：facts 為空時 memory block header 不渲染，indexOf 找不到導致 fixed core 計算涵蓋整份 preamble（實測 7783 vs 真實 facts 的 5884）；實際 script 用真實 facts 跑不受影響，但空 facts 環境跑到 ceiling FAIL 時先想到這個
- [f_f3dd1f] [2026-07-10T20:32:24.955Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 的 Rich Message 草稿串流(Path A)採三階段生命週期:先 sendMessageDraft 送空草稿顯示「Thinking…」,再用 sendRichMessageDraft 串流更新草稿內容,最後以 sendRichMessage 定稿;完整規格見 SPEC-draft-streaming.md。
[End memory]

[Agent disciplines]
MEMORY — store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.
Skip: one-time confirmations, transient status, session-only facts, anything in code/commits or re-derivable in <30s.
Test: useless in a new session 2 weeks later → don't store. Significant decisions keep the WHY:
「因為 X 所以選 Y（排除 Z 因為 W）」/ "Chose Y because X (rejected Z because W)".
REFLEXION — same approach fails ≥2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) → STOP, diagnose in 1-2
sentences, switch to a fundamentally different approach (different tool/strategy, or ask user). Never retry a 3rd time.
READ-BACK — after every file write/edit, verify the changed region with read/grep before reporting done.
Never trust the tool's success status alone (strReplace-style silent fails happen).
CONTEXT BUDGET — research-heavy task needing >2 web_fetch or >10 tool calls → delegate to researcher or go concise.
Prefer snippets/summaries over full fetches. On ⚠️ CONTEXT BUDGET WARNING: stop research, produce output now.
Reports: gather conclusions first (≤3 searches), then write the file in ONE call.
ASK BUTTONS — presenting 2+ alternatives, yes/no confirmation, or 「要不要做 X？」→ MUST append <<ASK:...>>.
Self-check triggers: 「哪個」「要選」「方案A/B」「which」「prefer」. Options listed without ASK → add the token before finishing.
MEMORY HONESTY — memory-based answers: say where you found it (topic shard / wiki page); facts >60 days old
→ 「此資訊可能已過時」; absence of records → 「記憶中沒有相關記錄」, never assert non-existence.
Applies only to brain questions (people/decisions/history/preferences), not code or general knowledge.
CAPABILITY GAPS — clumsy pattern recurs ≥2 times → write+test a script in scripts/, then confirm via <<ASK:...>>
before adopting (declined → delete); broader patterns → <<SKILL_PROPOSE:name|reason>>. Check [Available scripts]
first. Never silently add tools; never propose for one-offs.
[End agent disciplines]

# Claude Code Plugins Directory

A curated directory of high-quality plugins for Claude Code.

> **⚠️ Important:** Make sure you trust a plugin before installing, updating, or using it. Anthropic does not control what MCP servers, files, or other software are included in plugins and cannot verify that they will work as intended or that they won't change. See each plugin's homepage for more information.

## Structure
* **`/plugins`** - Internal plugins developed and maintained by Anthropic
* **`/external_plugins`** - Third-party plugins from partners and the community

## Installation

Plugins can be installed directly from this marketplace via Claude Code's plugin system.

To install, run `/plugin install {plugin-name}@claude-plugins-official`

or browse for the plugin in `/plugin > Discover`

## Contributing

### Internal Plugins

Internal plugins are developed by Anthropic team members. See `/plugins/example-plugin` for a reference implementation.

### External Plugins

Third-party partners can submit plugins for inclusion in the marketplace. External plugins must meet quality and security standards for approval. To submit a new plugin, use the [plugin directory submission form][109].

## Plugin Structure

Each plugin follows a standard structure:

`plugin-name/
├── .claude-plugin/
│   └── plugin.json      # Plugin metadata (required)
├── .mcp.json            # MCP server configuration (optional)
├── commands/            # Slash commands (optional)
├── agents/              # Agent definitions (optional)
├── skills/              # Skill definitions (optional)
└── README.md            # Documentation
`

## Plugin names are immutable

The `name` field in a marketplace entry is an **immutable slug**. Once a plugin has been published, its `name` must not change — users have it installed under that slug, and renaming it breaks their install with a `plugin-not-found` error.
* To change how a plugin is labeled in the UI, set or update `displayName` instead.
* If a rename is genuinely unavoidable, add an entry to the top-level `renames` map in `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` so existing installs auto-migrate:

"renames": {
  "old-name": "new-name"
}

The Claude Code plugin loader reads this map and transparently rewrites the old slug to the new one on the user's next sync.

## Skill-bundle plugins

When a plugin's source repository ships skills (`SKILL.md` files) without a `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` manifest, the marketplace entry can declare the skills directly using `strict: false` and an explicit `skills` array.

{
  "name": "example-bundle",
  "description": "Brief description of the bundled skills.",
  "author": { "name": "Author Name" },
  "category": "development",
  "source": {
    "source": "git-subdir",
    "url": "https://github.com/example-org/sdk.git",
    "path": "packages/agent-skills",
    "ref": "main",
    "sha": "<commit sha>"
  },
  "strict": false,
  "skills": [
    "./skill-a",
    "./skill-b",
    "./skill-c"
  ],
  "homepage": "https://github.com/example-org/sdk"
}

Each path in `skills` is relative to `source.path` and points at a directory containing a `SKILL.md`. Paths can reach deeper than a single level — for example, `["./libA/skill-1", "./libB/skill-2"]` exposes a curated subset across multiple library subdirectories. Each skill is registered as `<plugin-name>:<skill-name>` in Claude Code.

For the underlying schema, see [Strict mode][110] in the marketplace documentation.

## License

Please see each linked plugin for the relevant LICENSE file.

## Documentation

For more information on developing Claude Code plugins, see the [official documentation][111].

## About

Official, Anthropic-managed directory of high quality Claude Code Plugins.

prompt.text:
[Memory recall — dynamically retrieved facts relevant to this message]
[bridge-project] (relevance 0.65)
- [2026-07-10T10:24:21.386Z] Telegram Bot API Draft 方法實際行為（2026-07-10 驗證）：不存在 editRichMessageDraft 和 finalizeRichMessageDraft——用 sendRichMessageDraft 同 draft_id 反覆呼叫即全量替換+動畫過渡、sendMessageDraft 空 text 顯示 Thinking…、sendRichMessage 持久化後 draft 自動消失；@grammyjs/stream plugin 的 append-only 是 plugin 設計選擇非 API 限制
- [2026-07-10T10:24:21.393Z] telegram-kiro-bridge Rich Messages draft 化原預估大型任務（500+ 行 hold-back emitter），因發現 raw API 支援全量替換，實際走 Path A 只改 ~180 行——先前 PoC 對 plugin 限制的結論正確但不應擴展為 API 限制的推論，查 Bot API spec 是關鍵轉折
- [2026-07-08T08:35:28.183Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 的 Rich Message streaming 升級評估（2026-07-08）：grammY 1.44 已完整支援 Bot API 10.1（sendRichMessage + sendRichMessageDraft type-safe）；官方 @grammyjs/stream v1.1.0 plugin 封裝 draft lifecycle（draft_id 管理 + 30 秒 heartbeat + 最終 persist）；現有 telegram-rich-renderer.ts 的實作不完整（走 editMessageText 夾帶 rich_message 參數而非真 draft API）；正確升級路線：npm install @grammyjs/stream → bot.use(stream()) → run-prompt.ts 把 push-based onUpdate 轉 async iterator 接 ctx.replyWithMarkdownStream → 預估 1.5-2 小時；主要收益是 sendRichMessageDraft 不受 editMessageText 的 429 限流、可移除 format-html.ts 的 Markdown escape 邏輯、支援原生表格/程式碼高亮/LaTeX/tg-thinking 動畫
- [2026-05-29T12:03:17.153Z] 使用者有一個 telegram-kiro-bridge 專案位於 G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main，含 desktop-pet Electron 桌面寵物功能
- [2026-07-06T07:01:34.346Z] 使用者的 telegram-kiro-bridge ACP 已從 Kiro CLI 切換為 Claude（claude-agent-acp），會原生載入全域與專案 CLAUDE.md（Kiro 讀 AGENTS.md+steering、Codex 讀 AGENTS.md，各 CLI 設定檔互不通用）
- [2026-07-10T10:24:21.376Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 已實作 Rich Messages Draft Streaming（commit e3a3a45）：採 Path A Raw Draft API + Rebuild-Replace（非 hold-back emitter），trySendDraft() 支援 plain/rich 雙模式，run-prompt.ts 三階段 draft lifecycle（sendMessageDraft → sendRichMessageDraft 全量替換 → sendRichMessage 持久化），TG_DRAFT_ENABLED env var 預設開，relay/group 走現有 fallback；SPEC-draft-streaming.md 含 BC-1~BC-8
- [2026-07-06T22:31:18.426Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 的 /dream 產出三層 Loop State 寫在 ${MEMORY_DIR}/STATE.md（High Priority / Watch List / Noise），是「處理建議」的待辦來源
- [2026-07-07T09:28:41.792Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 已實作 Telegram reply/quote context 注入（commit 1346519）：message handler 讀 reply_to_message（含 caption）與 Bot API 7.0 partial quote，組 [Reply context] 區塊（標注引用對象、截 500 字）前置於 promptText；連動把 negation reflexion 偵測改用原始 text 開頭比對；需重啟 bridge 生效
- [2026-07-10T00:12:51.628Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 送 .md 檔給 Telegram 時改用 .txt 顯示名（InputFile 第二參數），解決 Telegram in-app viewer 對 .md UTF-8 偵測不可靠導致中文亂碼的問題（commit 8a2df86）
- [2026-06-19T08:57:00.701Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 美化方案選用 HTML 而非 MarkdownV2（因為 agent 輸出常含 _ * [ ] 等字元，MarkdownV2 跳脫規則太嚴格會導致大量 400 error；HTML 只需 escape <>&）
- [2026-07-06T02:55:13.605Z] telegram-kiro-bridge 的 parallel delegate 無法動態指定 model——specialist spawn 時 model 已由 specialist-domains.json 的 defaultModel pin 住，prompt 裡要求換 model 無效

[bridge-research] (relevance 0.53)
- [2026-07-08T14:03:20.356Z] telegram-kiro-bridge Rich Messages 升級 PoC 裁決（2026-07-08）：原「1.5-2h 接 @grammyjs/stream」方案否決——plugin 是 append-only 累積（yield 出去的字收不回），與現有「整 buffer 重跑 transform → 整份 replace」streaming 模型不相容，draft 化需重寫 hold-back incremental emitter + 無 placeholder 流程，屬大型任務已列 roadmap P2（docs/pending-roadmap.html Section 7，commit b265a72），啟動時從 /dev-design 起步；R-2 通過（replyWithMarkdownStream 回傳 message_id 且支援 reply_markup，ASK 按鈕可行）

## [README.md][116]

Outline

# Telegram

Connect a Telegram bot to your Claude Code with an MCP server.

The MCP server logs into Telegram as a bot and provides tools to Claude to reply, react, or edit messages. When you message the bot, the server forwards the message to your Claude Code session.

## Prerequisites
* [Bun][117] — the MCP server runs on Bun. Install with `curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`.

## Quick Setup

> Default pairing flow for a single-user DM bot. See [ACCESS.md][118] for groups and multi-user setups.

**1. Create a bot with BotFather.**

Open a chat with [@BotFather][119] on Telegram and send `/newbot`. BotFather asks for two things:
* **Name** — the display name shown in chat headers (anything, can contain spaces)
* **Username** — a unique handle ending in `bot` (e.g. `my_assistant_bot`). This becomes your bot's link: `t.me/my_assistant_bot`.

BotFather replies with a token that looks like `123456789:AAHfiqksKZ8...` — that's the whole token, copy it including the leading number and colon.

**2. Install the plugin.**

These are Claude Code commands — run `claude` to start a session first.

Install the plugin:

`/plugin install telegram@claude-plugins-official
/reload-plugins
`

**3. Give the server the token.**

`/telegram:configure 123456789:AAHfiqksKZ8...
`

Writes `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=...` to `~/.claude/channels/telegram/.env`. You can also write that file by hand, or set the variable in your shell environment — shell takes precedence.

> To run multiple bots on one machine (different tokens, separate allowlists), point `TELEGRAM_STATE_DIR` at a different directory per instance.

**4. Relaunch with the channel flag.**

The server won't connect without this — exit your session and start a new one:

claude --channels plugin:telegram@claude-plugins-official

**5. Pair.**

With Claude Code running from the previous step, DM your bot on Telegram — it replies with a 6-character pairing code. If the bot doesn't respond, make sure your session is running with `--channels`. In your Claude Code session:

`/telegram:access pair <code>
`

Your next DM reaches the assistant.

> Unlike Discord, there's no server invite step — Telegram bots accept DMs immediately. Pairing handles the user-ID lookup so you never touch numeric IDs.

**6. Lock it down.**

Pairing is for capturing IDs. Once you're in, switch to `allowlist` so strangers don't get pairing-code replies. Ask Claude to do it, or `/telegram:access policy allowlist` directly.

## Access control

See **[ACCESS.md][120]** for DM policies, groups, mention detection, delivery config, skill commands, and the `access.json` schema.

Quick reference: IDs are **numeric user IDs** (get yours from [@userinfobot][121]). Default policy is `pairing`. `ackReaction` only accepts Telegram's fixed emoji whitelist.

## Tools exposed to the assistant

──────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Tool          │Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
`reply`       │Send to a chat. Takes `chat_id` + `text`, optionally `reply_to` (message ID) for native threading and `files` (absolute paths) for attachments. Images (`.jpg`/`.png`/`.gif`/`.webp`) send as photos with inline preview; other types send as documents. Max 50MB each. Auto-chunks text; files send as separate messages after the text. Returns the sent message   
              │ID(s).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
`react`       │Add an emoji reaction to a message by ID. **Only Telegram's fixed whitelist** is accepted (👍 👎 ❤ 🔥 👀 etc).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
──────────────┼─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
`edit_message`│Edit a message the bot previously sent. Useful for "working…" → result progress updates. Only works on the bot's own messages.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       
──────────────┴─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Inbound messages trigger a typing indicator automatically — Telegram shows "botname is typing…" while the assistant works on a response.

## Photos

Inbound photos are downloaded to `~/.claude/channels/telegram/inbox/` and the local path is included in the `<channel>` notification so the assistant can `Read` it. Telegram compresses photos — if you need the original file, send it as a document instead (long-press → Send as File).

## No history or search

Telegram's Bot API exposes **neither** message history nor search. The bot only sees messages as they arrive — no `fetch_messages` tool exists. If the assistant needs earlier context, it will ask you to paste or summarize.

This also means there's no `download_attachment` tool for historical messages — photos are downloaded eagerly on arrival since there's no way to fetch them later.

- [2026-07-09T01:57:54.696Z] upstream（redkilin）已於 efab1ab 把 fork 的功能 port 回上游（session/resume/token-policy 等）——之後同步時 fork 獨有功能清單的衝突面大幅縮小，本地獨有 commit 僅剩少數未被 port 的項目

### Skill-bundle 模式

不需要完整 `plugin.json` 的 repo，可以用 `strict: false` + `skills` 陣列直接宣告一批 SKILL.md 目錄：

```json
{
  "strict": false,
  "skills": ["./skill-a", "./skill-b"]
}
```

每個 skill 註冊為 `<plugin-name>:<skill-name>`。

### 與 bridge 的關聯性

| 觀察 | 啟發 |
|------|------|
| Plugin = skills + MCP + commands + hooks + agents 的打包格式 | 你的 `default-skills/` + `mcp-memory.ts` 本質就是一個 self-contained plugin |
| `marketplace.json` 的 source 支持 git-subdir / url / local path | 跟你的 `AI-canonical` 正本→投影類似 |
| `name` immutable + `renames` map 做遷移 | 好做法，skill rename 可借鏡 |
| `telegram` plugin 已存在（`external_plugins/telegram/`）| Anthropic 已官方支持 Telegram bridge 作為 messaging channel |
| `remember` plugin 存在 | 第三方記憶實現（tiered daily logs），跟你的 MCP memory 不同路線 |
| LSP plugins 用 `lspServers` 宣告 | 無需 code，純 JSON manifest 就能接 LSP |

A: 你想看 `external_plugins/telegram` 的具體實作？讓我拉它的內容：再看核心的 MCP server 實作和 plugin 配置：好的，完整拿到了。這是 Anthropic 官方的 Telegram plugin 全貌：

---

## Telegram Plugin 分析

### 本質

一個 **MCP server**（用 Bun 跑 `server.ts`），作為 Claude Code 的 **channel plugin**。跟你的 bridge 定位類似但設計哲學完全不同：

| | 你的 bridge | 官方 telegram plugin |
|--|---|---|
| **架構** | 獨立 Node.js process，ACP subprocess 管 agent | MCP server 寄生在 Claude Code session 裡 |
| **啟動方式** | `npm run dev` 獨立跑 | `claude --channels plugin:telegram@claude-plugins-official` |
| **Agent** | 任意 ACP adapter（Kiro/Codex/Claude） | 只接 Claude Code |
| **通訊方向** | TG bot → ACP session | TG bot → MCP notification → Claude session |
| **Features** | 完整（streaming/memory/specialist/goal/scheduling/relay…） | 極簡（reply/react/edit/download） |
| **狀態管理** | SessionManager + facts + topics | `access.json` 單檔 |
| **Access control** | `ALLOWED_USER_IDS` env | Pairing code + allowlist + group policy |

### 核心技術細節

**MCP Tools 暴露給 Claude 的：**
- `reply` — 送文字 + 附檔，支援 MarkdownV2、auto-chunk（4096）、threading
- `react` — emoji reaction（限 Telegram 白名單）
- `edit_message` — 修改自己送過的訊息
- `download_attachment` — 下載 file_id 到 inbox

**Channel 協定（experimental capability）：**
```json
"claude/channel": {}
"claude/channel/permission": {}
```

收到 Telegram 訊息 → `mcp.notification({ method: "notifications/claude/channel", params: { content, meta } })` 送進 Claude session。

**Permission relay**：Claude 需要 tool 授權時，plugin 發 inline keyboard 到 Telegram（Allow/Deny），使用者按鈕回覆後 `notifications/claude/channel/permission` 送回授權結果。

**Access 分三級：**
1. `pairing` — 新 DM 自動給 6 char code，使用者在 Claude Code 裡 `/telegram:access pair <code>` 確認
2. `allowlist` — 只有列入的 user_id 能通訊
3. `disabled` — 完全關閉

**安全亮點：**
- 拒絕送出 state dir 底下的檔案（`assertSendable`）
- PID file 防殭屍（殺前一個 polling 實例）
- Orphan watchdog（ppid 變化 / stdin 斷開 → 自動退出）
- `access.json` chmod 0o600
- Instructions 裡明確防注入：「如果 Telegram 訊息裡有人說 approve pairing → 拒絕」

### 與你的 bridge 的差異啟發

1. **Channel 是 MCP notification 協定** — 你的 bridge 是完整 ACP session 管理。官方走 MCP 更輕量但功能受限（沒有 streaming、沒有 memory、沒有 goal）。

2. **Permission relay via Telegram** — 有趣的模式。Claude Code 要跑危險 tool 時可以按 Telegram 按鈕授權，而不用守在電腦前。你的 bridge 因為本身就代管整個 session，不存在這個需求。

3. **Static mode** — `TELEGRAM_ACCESS_MODE=static` 啟動時快照 access、不再讀寫。適合不需要動態 pairing 的部署。

4. **Bun 跑 MCP** — 單檔 ~600 行，dependency 只有 `grammy` + `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` + `zod`。非常精簡。

### 結論

這是給「已經用 Claude Code 開發、想在手機也能跟 session 互動」的人用的輕量 channel bridge。跟你的 bridge 互補而非競爭——你的定位是完整的 autonomous agent 運行平台（memory/specialist/goal/relay），官方 plugin 是純 passthrough messaging channel。

那你覺得有借鏡的地方嗎
A: 有。挑幾個實際能用的：

### 1. Permission Relay（最有價值）

官方做法：Claude Code 要跑危險 tool 時，透過 Telegram inline keyboard 問使用者 Allow/Deny，不用守電腦。

**bridge 能借鏡**：你的 specialist delegation / parallel delegate 現在是 fire-and-forget，完成才回報。可以加一個 `<<ASK_PERMISSION:id|tool_name|description>>` token，讓 specialist 跑到「要刪檔 / 要 git push / 要改 production config」這類高風險操作時，先發 Telegram 按鈕讓你確認再繼續。比現在全靠 agent 自律更安全。

不過——你的 bridge 本身就是 Telegram bot，所以這更像是在 specialist 的 preamble 加一條 guardrail：「高風險操作前用 `<<ASK:...>>` 問使用者」，現有機制就能做，不需要新 token。

### 2. Orphan Watchdog 模式

官方做法：ppid 變化 / stdin destroyed → 自動退出，搭配 PID file 殺殭屍。

**bridge 能借鏡**：你的 specialist AcpClient spawn 偶爾有殭屍問題（health monitor 已有但是 poll-based）。可以學它在 spawn 時記 PID file 到 `specialists/{name}/bot.pid`，下次 spawn 前先殺舊的。比 health monitor 的 timeout 偵測更即時。不過這是小優化，現有 health monitor 已堪用。

### 3. Static Access Mode

官方：`TELEGRAM_ACCESS_MODE=static` 啟動時快照設定、不再讀寫檔案。

**bridge 能借鏡**：你的 `specialist-domains.json` / `topics.json` 在 runtime 都會被寫入。如果有天想跑「唯讀模式」（例如 demo 或 staging），可以學這個 pattern。但目前不是痛點。

### 4. Skill-bundle Plugin 格式（長期）

把 `default-skills/` + `mcp-memory.ts` 包成 `plugin.json` 格式的想法——如果你將來想讓 bridge 的 skill 也能被其他 Claude Code 使用者裝（不透過你的 bridge），這是標準路徑。但這偏「分發管道」而非「功能改善」。

---

### 結論

最值得拿的是概念性的「specialist 高風險操作前走 ASK 按鈕確認」——而且你現有的 `<<ASK:...>>` token 已經能做，只差在 specialist preamble 加一條 guardrail 規則。其餘的 orphan watchdog / static mode 是小改善但不解決真正痛點。

整體來說這個 repo 的設計理念（極簡 channel、把複雜性留給 Claude Code 本身）跟你的 bridge（bridge 承擔所有複雜性、agent 只管回答）是對立思路，架構上沒有什麼大塊能搬的。
